goal: Freeze the verified FCL architecture and production-closure evidence for the Files-local Locations rail, explicit origin authority, bounded directory I/O, responsive rail/drawer geometry, and preserved fractional Miller Trail scrolling.

project.count_authority: The fresh single-worker CLI result is authoritative. The long-lived MCP status channel retained the previous edge count, so ready alone is not treated as freshness evidence.

ontology.type: client-local Files presentation and bounded-I/O dependency graph
in_scope: Files-local Locations rail and compact drawer
root, Miller navigation, operation refresh, and watcher refresh I/O
out_of_scope: stable Herdr process or socket mutation
release assets and public release docs

components.name: global shell tracker
components.role: Files activation preserves the global Spaces/Projects agent and workspace projection.

components.name: FileManagerLocationOrigin
components.role: Carries exact Location(path) or Direct(path) identity without cwd or longest-prefix inference.

components.name: FileManagerLocationsState
components.role: Owns rail focus, bounded vertical offset, explicit origin, pending/failure projection, and compact drawer state.

components.name: FileManagerIoWorker
components.role: Runs one executing plus one latest-pending root/navigation/refresh request off input, render, and scheduled-apply threads.

components.name: FileManagerIoIdentity::is_current
components.role: Requires exact Files generation, model revision, source identity, request generation, and target identity before apply.

components.name: sync_file_manager_location_request
components.role: Validates accessible exact location rows, reuses resident roots without I/O, or submits typed root work to the bounded worker.

components.name: schedule_file_manager_watcher_at
components.role: Routes current/watcher refresh through the same prepared I/O and rejects stale completion authority.

components.name: file_manager_content_layout
components.role: Projects Wide, Standard, or Compact Files geometry with disjoint rail, separator, action/drawer, and Trail rectangles.

components.name: render_file_manager_locations
components.role: Renders only prepared locations rows, exact origin, pending/failure state, and the compact drawer.

components.name: App::handle_file_manager_mouse
components.role: Revalidates current typed hit identities; rail vertical input and Trail fractional horizontal input remain rectangle-local.

components.name: render_trail_view
components.role: Consumes only the remaining Trail rectangle and preserves the approved one-third horizontal movement.

components.name: files-locations.spec.ts
components.role: Verifies eight ASCII deterministic wide, origin, standard, compact, pending, and failure compositions.

claims.text: Opening Files does not replace the global agent/workspace tracker; Favorites and Locations are Files-content ownership.
claims.evidence: FCL focused tests 29/29 and full Rust 3577/3577.

claims.text: Highlight authority is explicit Location(path) or Direct(path), survives descendant navigation, and transfers only on explicit activation.
claims.evidence: Graph found FileManagerLocationOrigin; TP-FCL-AUTH tests and VIS-19/VIS-20 pass.

claims.text: Root, Miller navigation, operation refresh, and watcher/current refresh use one bounded latest-pending worker lane.
claims.evidence: Graph found FileManagerIoWorker, sync_file_manager_location_request, schedule_file_manager_watcher_at, and fcl_io_miller_and_current_refresh_share_worker_lane.

claims.text: Missing, changed-type, permission, panic, disconnect, close/reopen, direct activation, and model replacement cannot replace resident Trail with stale work.
claims.evidence: Focused FCL worker family 29/29 and full Rust 3577/3577.

claims.text: Wide and Standard expose persistent rail geometry; Compact exposes one complete action and bounded top drawer; all rectangles are bounded and disjoint.
claims.evidence: Graph reports file_manager_content_layout in-degree 3, complexity 3; TP-FCL-GEO and drawer tests pass.

claims.text: Locations render only from prepared state; global legacy Files body render/hit-test/navigation seams are absent.
claims.evidence: Legacy source residue search returned no matches and Chromium VIS-18..25 pass.

claims.text: Fresh exact primary clicks route once; modifiers, gaps, separator, inaccessible paths, stale rows, and drawer background are inert.
claims.evidence: Focused input/drawer tests pass and headless raw mouse location navigation remains covered by full Rust.

claims.text: Locations vertical scroll and Miller fractional horizontal scroll have disjoint rectangle authority.
Eight new deterministic ASCII fixtures pass; a one-cell mutation fails only VIS-18 by two pixels; restored full Chromium passes 33/33.
claims.evidence: RED 7c743e06, GREEN 8dba99d3, baseline SHA 174f8e6e7db2b3a052d19d6f14defc15e01621ead2c0806318409f043b2508a2, mutated SHA 2a99424c096dbe4c38ed8d340593bde1133b4d268fd5485c498234f3314c08ac.

verifications.result: bulk recovery exact test 20/20 after explicit equal mtime; full suite pass

failure_memory.attempt: Treat every unwrap match in the new worker modules as production residue.
failure_memory.result: Rejected because the raw search included the cfg(test) module.
failure_memory.prevention: Split at the exact #[cfg(test)] mod tests boundary, not the first cfg(test) attribute.

failure_memory.attempt: Use the plan's scripts/tests and integrations/claude maintenance paths.
failure_memory.result: Rejected because both paths are absent in the current repository.
failure_memory.prevention: Read the lowercase justfile and execute its exact current direct equivalents when just is unavailable.

failure_memory.attempt: Assume bulk recovery fixture write order remains selection order.
failure_memory.result: Rejected by strict mixed-mtime sorting; sequential writes can reorder rows before request validation.
failure_memory.prevention: Assign selected fixture files one explicit equal mtime and retain strict production order validation.

variant.optional_manual_acceptance: Not run in this automated closure because it requires a user-driven host input session; stable Herdr and user sessions remained untouched.
